sections.paragraph: & עֻזִּיָּהוּ (“power of Jehovah”), pr.n. Uzziah, king of Judah, from 811-759 B.C., 2Ki 15:13, 30 2Ki 15:30, 32 2Ki 15:32, 34 2Ki 15:34 Isa 1:1 6:1 7:1 Hos 1:1 Amo 1:1. In 2Ki 14:21 15:1 , 2Ki 15:6, 2Ki 15:8, 23 2Ki 15:23, 27 2Ki 15:27, he is called also עֲזַרְיָה and עֲזַרְיָהוּ; which I should attribute not to a two-fold name of the same king, but to an error of copyists (as עזיה and עזריה are alike), or to an interchange of the names as spoken by the common people (ss being pronounced for sr). Comp. No. 3.-(2) 1Ch 27:25.-(3) 1Ch 6:9 for which there is in verse 21 1Ch 6:21 עֲזַרְיָה.-(4) Ezr 10:21.-(5) Neh 11:4. LXX. Ὀζίας.
